title: Rocks Comes to Elbaf
description: On pages 5–7, this part follows Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Rocks D. Xebec as The story turns toward Elbaf’s past, Loki, Rocks, Harald, and the ancient grudges shaping the island’s present conflict.
scene: The story turns toward Elbaf’s past, Loki, Rocks, Harald, and the ancient grudges shaping the island’s present conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: THE MAN CLAIMED TO BE A DAY JONESIAN PIRATE...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He knew there was nothing more he could do in that moment,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So he withdrew with these words:

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Just wait. One day...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I'll be back!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Naturally, this incident would never...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: ...be brought to light.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ironically, on that very day, there was another intruder in the Holy Land.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Look!! It's so much bigger than a normal giant!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I want it as a slave!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No, we should kill it and have it stuffed!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: guards, protect the nobles!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot Hot

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And I know trespassing is a crime, but isn't all this going a bit too far?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Don't let the "Kingnapper" get away!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: That wasn't me, I swear!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: An Admiral was murdered!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The culprit ran towards the castle, proceed with caution!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: It's just one thing after the other!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Seize that big'un!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That must be the guy who kidnapped the kings!! How dare he use me as a scapegoat?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I'll show him...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A giant in the Holy Land? Don't your kind lurk in the boonies?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Voha HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA! State YOUR NAME!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: BARA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA! It's HARald!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The clash between the two strangers...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...blew away everyone within a 5 kilometer radius.

dialogue.speaker: Rocks D. Xebec
dialogue.text: I'm rocks!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Angle your next strike toward the town, they'll ensure you if you get caught!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Huh?! Why?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Don't ask questions!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: WARM!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: G'HAM!!

summary: This part advances the Elbaf’s Buried History through 33 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
